message:
🐛 Fix ~1 minute delay when switching dashboards after backend restart (#804)

Root cause: The frontend's 5-second API timeout fires before the backend
finishes cold-starting K8s cluster connections. The AbortError handler
then calls markBackendFailure(), which sets a 60-second lockout period
(BACKEND_CHECK_INTERVAL) during which NO API calls are attempted.

Three fixes:
- Remove markBackendFailure() from AbortError handler in GET requests.
  A slow request does not mean the backend is down. Only actual network
  errors (fetch TypeError = backend unreachable) should mark it failed.
  This matches the POST method which already doesn't mark failure on timeout.
- Increase DEFAULT_TIMEOUT from 5s to 15s to give cold K8s connections
  time to complete (TLS handshake + API discovery + health aggregation).
- Reduce BACKEND_CHECK_INTERVAL from 60s to 10s so recovery is faster
  if the backend is temporarily slow rather than truly dead.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
